summary:
Iran and the US wrapped up technical talks in Switzerland with both sides claiming progress toward a nuclear deal. Iran's top negotiator, Mohammad Ghalibaf, said Tehran agreed to allow international inspections of its nuclear sites. In exchange, the US will release roughly $12 billion in frozen Iranian assets. The talks are the highest-level direct contact between the two countries in years, and mediators say the framework of a broader agreement is taking shape — though details on timing and verification remain unresolved.

A gas explosion at Qatar's main LNG processing plant in Ras Laffan killed at least 13 people and wounded dozens more. Qatari authorities say it was a technical accident, not a deliberate attack, and have opened an investigation. The facility handles about a third of the world's liquefied natural gas trade, so any prolonged shutdown could ripple through global energy markets. Meanwhile, in northern India, a building fire killed 15 people; witnesses reported seeing residents jump from windows to escape. Authorities have ordered an inquiry.

Colombia's presidential runoff ended with a razor-thin victory for far-right lawyer Abelardo de la Espriella, who took 49.66% of the vote against leftist Iván Cepeda's 48.7%. Cepeda alleged irregularities in the count, but international observers have not reported widespread fraud. De la Espriella, a Trump-endorsed outsider, has promised to reverse the country's peace deal with FARC rebels and crack down on crime — a sharp policy shift for a nation still emerging from decades of armed conflict.

A UN commission reported that Israel deliberately killed Palestinian children during the war, including after a ceasefire took effect in October 2025, and said the actions amount to genocide. Israel has rejected the findings as biased. Separately, the EU held high-level talks with Taliban officials in Brussels on returning failed Afghan asylum-seekers — one of the bloc's most significant contacts with the Taliban since 2021. And in the South China Sea, four Chinese warships confronted a Philippine Navy vessel near Scarborough Shoal on Saturday, as Manila wrapped up a joint military drill with the US and other allies. Beijing has not commented.

headlines.title: Iran technical talks with US in Switzerland conclude successfully
headlines.summary: Iran and the US wrapped up technical talks in Switzerland, with Iran agreeing to allow nuclear inspections and the US easing $12 billion in frozen assets, marking progress toward a war-ending deal.

framings.angle: Emphasized successful conclusion and asset release agreement
Highlighted Pezeshkian's Pakistan visit alongside deal details
Focused on Iran denying new commitments on nuclear sites

headlines.title: At least 13 killed and dozens injured after Qatar gas explosion
headlines.summary: A technical accident at Qatar's main LNG processing site in Ras Laffan killed at least 13 people and injured dozens. Authorities are investigating the cause of the explosion.

headlines.title: U.N. inquiry reports Israel targeted Palestinian children, amounting to genocide
headlines.summary: A U.N. commission reported that Israel deliberately killed Palestinian children during the war, even after a ceasefire in October 2025. The finding calls for international accountability.

headlines.title: Far-right millionaire Abelardo de la Espriella wins Colombia presidential runoff
headlines.summary: Trump-endorsed lawyer Abelardo de la Espriella won Colombia's presidential runoff with 49.66% of votes, defeating leftist Iván Cepeda. Cepeda alleged irregularities; de la Espriella's victory marks a sharp policy shift.

headlines.title: Inquiry ordered after building fire kills 15 in northern India city
headlines.summary: A building fire in a northern Indian city killed 15 people, with witnesses reporting people jumping from windows. Authorities ordered an inquiry as rescue teams saved others.

headlines.title: EU hosts Taliban officials for migrant return talks in Brussels
headlines.summary: The EU held high-level talks with Taliban officials in Brussels to discuss returning failed Afghan asylum-seekers, granting temporary visas for the meeting. It is one of the bloc's highest-level contacts since 2021.

headlines.title: China and Philippines in rare naval stand-off near Scarborough Shoal
headlines.summary: Four Chinese warships confronted a Philippine Navy vessel near Scarborough Shoal on Saturday, as Manila ended a joint drill with the US and allies. Beijing has not commented on the incident.

headlines.title: Whistleblower investigating Ecuador president's family business murdered, activists say
headlines.summary: Polish activist Monika Silva Koniuszek, who investigated the Ecuadorian president's family business, died from a blow to the head and strangulation, according to a postmortem. Activists say she was murdered to silence her.

headlines.title: Japan top court upholds dissolution order for Unification Church
headlines.summary: Japan's Supreme Court upheld the dissolution order for the Unification Church, ruling it does not violate constitutional guarantees of religious freedom. The decision is final.

headlines.title: Montreal shooting leaves officer, civilian and suspect dead
headlines.summary: A shooting in Montreal killed a police officer, a civilian, and the suspect, marking the first line-of-duty death of a Montreal police officer in 24 years, the police chief said.

headlines.title: Iran moves to assert control over Strait of Hormuz, plans to charge fees
headlines.summary: Iran, after making the Strait of Hormuz unsafe for business, now plans to charge fees for vessel transit. Experts say this is a move to control the strategic waterway.
headlines.previousContext: Iran has previously weaponized the Strait of Hormuz in the ongoing conflict, disrupting shipping. Today, it announced plans to charge transit fees.
